critic_raw:
Round 1 Critique:

1. (global_auc_not_decision_metric) AUC over the full distribution is the wrong primary metric when only 2% of users will ever receive the intervention. Performance gains outside the top 2% are irrelevant to business value.

2. (no_capacity_constrained_evaluation) Precision@2%, lift@2%, and recall@2% are absent. They are the direct measures of interest and are computable from existing test data.

3. (prediction_metric_not_intervention_metric) Incremental retention among called users is not reported. This is the ultimate business metric.
defender_raw:
Round 1 Defense:

1. The AUC difference is large enough (7 points) to be practically significant at any reasonable operating point. The threshold-independence of AUC is a feature: it does not penalize the model for threshold sensitivity in score distributions.

2. Top-k metrics are easy to add. They can be computed from the held-out month data. The omission does not invalidate the model's quality.

3. Incremental retention requires a controlled experiment. The study period has a fixed call script and offer menu — this controls for intervention quality, but without a holdout it is difficult to isolate model-driven incremental retention from baseline retention.
adjudication_raw:
After Round 1:
Point 1 (global_auc_not_decision_metric): OPEN — both sides acknowledge the metric-business alignment gap; disagree on its severity.
Point 2 (no_capacity_constrained_evaluation): RESOLVED — defender acknowledges the gap and notes it is easily filled. Record as acknowledged limitation; empirical fill required.
Point 3 (prediction_metric_not_intervention_metric): OPEN — defender correctly notes causal estimation requires experimental design.

Round 2:
Critic Mode 2 on Point 1: The approval request is to switch the weekly retention workflow immediately. This is a production decision that should be supported by metrics aligned with production use. AUC is a development metric. The gap between AUC and top-k metrics can be large in practice, particularly when class imbalance is high (churn prevalence is 6%).

Defender Mode 2 on Point 1: At 6% prevalence, ROC-AUC is still informative. Precision-recall curves would be more appropriate at high imbalance — but again, the 7-point AUC lift is a strong signal. The concern is theoretical in the absence of contrary evidence.

Point 1: Force-resolved as empirical_test_required — compute precision@2% from test set before approving the workflow switch.

Point 3 force-resolved: recommend a randomized A/B test at initial deployment rather than full workflow switch, to enable measurement of incremental retention lift.

Final verdict: empirical_test_agreed

empirical_test.measure: precision and lift at top-2% cutoff on the held-out test month
empirical_test.success_criterion: new model precision@2% and lift@2% exceed incumbent logistic model by a meaningful margin consistent with the AUC improvement
empirical_test.failure_criterion: new model precision@2% and lift@2% are comparable to or below incumbent despite the 7-point AUC improvement

points_resolved: no_capacity_constrained_evaluation (acknowledged gap, computable from test data)
